title: Veizik Run Report v1
description: Optional performance and compatibility report emitted by the Veizik CLI after a render completes. Sent only with explicit Category B consent (see TELEMETRY.md). This schema is an allowlist: additionalProperties is false at every level, so any field not named here is a validation failure rather than silently accepted data. The server rejects reports whose privacy object is not four false values.

schema_version.description: Schema discriminator. Ingestion routes on this value.

event_id.description: Unique id for this report, used for idempotent ingestion and de-duplication of retried batches.

installation_id.description: Pseudonymous installation identifier (hex hash). Not anonymous: it is stable and may be associable with a license, and is therefore treated as pseudonymized personal data under PIPA and the GDPR. This is the key used to honor deletion requests.

license_tier.description: Plan under which the run executed.

telemetry_consent_version.description: Version of the optional-performance-data consent text the user accepted. Reports carrying a consent version that is no longer valid for the collected fields are rejected.

runtime.description: Which Veizik build and which loaded components executed the run.

adapter_id.description: Model Adapter identifier, or null when no adapter was loaded.

capsule_id.description: Capsule identifier, or null when the run was not capsule-driven.

execution_backend.description: Execution path used for this run.

profile_id.description: Hardware-aware runtime profile selected for this machine.

hardware.description: Machine capability class. Bucketed where a raw value would add identifiability without adding compatibility signal.

gpu_model.description: Marketing model string as reported by the driver, e.g. "NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090".

vram_mb.description: VRAM of a single GPU, in MiB.

cuda_version.description: CUDA runtime version, or null on non-CUDA machines.

os_major.description: OS family and major version only, e.g. "linux-6", "windows-11", "macos-15". Never a full build string.

cpu_class.description: CPU class rather than exact SKU, e.g. "x86_64-16c", "apple-m3-max".

ram_band.description: System RAM as a bucket, not an exact figure.

power_limit_w.description: GPU power cap in watts, when readable. Materially affects render throughput.

workload.description: What was asked of the runtime. Describes the shape of the job only; never its content.

model_id.description: Public model identifier, e.g. "ltx-video-13b". Never a local path and never a private or user-named model directory.

model_hash.description: Hash of the model weights, used to distinguish revisions of the same public id.

frames.description: Frame count. 1 or null for image workloads.

tile.description: Tiling configuration as a shape descriptor, e.g. "512x512", or "off".

concurrent_jobs.description: Number of Veizik jobs running on this node at dispatch time.

start_mode.description: Whether weights were already resident. Cold and warm runs are not comparable.

result.description: What actually happened. Failure reports are as valuable as successful ones and are collected the same way.

power_draw_w.description: Average GPU power draw in watts. Optional; omitted or null where unreadable.

error_code.description: Stable Veizik error code, e.g. "E_OOM_DENOISE". Never a raw exception message, stack trace, or path.

stopped_stage.description: Pipeline stage at which the run stopped, for failed or cancelled runs.

oom.description: True if the run hit an out-of-memory condition at any stage.

recovered.description: True if a Recover / TimeMachine path resumed the run after a fault.

user_cancelled.description: True if the operator interrupted the run.

product_signals.description: Optional coarse usage counters. Counters only; no content, no per-invocation arguments.

first_success.description: True on the report for this installation's first successful render. Also starts the Starter Preview trial window, which begins at first successful render rather than at activation.

feature_use_counts.description: Map of feature name to invocation count, e.g. {"queue": 12, "recover": 1}.

privacy.description: Client-side attestation that no user content is present in this report. Every member is const false. A report asserting otherwise is REJECTED at ingestion rather than sanitized and stored, so an accidental content leak becomes a hard failure instead of a silent data spill.

contains_prompt.description: Prompt or negative-prompt text. Must be false.

contains_input.description: Input image, video, or audio content. Must be false.

contains_output.description: Generated output, including thumbnails and single frames. Must be false.

contains_local_path.description: Filesystem paths, filenames, usernames, or hostnames. Must be false.
